zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Cole於1210年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親Roger Cole25歲，母親Edith Burdenville24歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1235年在英國英格蘭戴文享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Cole于1210年出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲Roger Cole25岁，母亲Edith Burdenville24岁。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1235年在英国英格兰戴文享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colton
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire): habitational name from any of various places called Colton in England. Examples in Norfolk, Staffordshire, and North Yorkshire are from the Old English personal name Cola (or the cognate Old Norse Koli; see Cole 2) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The place so named in Somerset has as its first element the Old English personal name Cūla (of uncertain origin). The one in Cumbria has a river name apparently derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘hazel’. This English name is also common in Ireland; it was the name of a bishop of Derry in 1397. There seems also to have been confusion with Culliton .
Scottish and Irish (Monaghan): shortened and altered form of Gaelic Mac Haldan, ‘son of Haldan’, see Haldane .

variants.name: Colclough
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Cheshire): habitational name from Cowclough in the parish of Whitworth, Lancashire, earlier recorded as Colleclogh, probably named with the Old English byname Cola (see Cole 2) + Old English clōh ‘ravine’.

variants.name: Calcote
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in Bedfordshire, Berkshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Northamptonshire, Warwickshire, and elsewhere) named Caldecote or Caldecott, from Old English cald ‘cold’ + cot ‘cottage, dwelling’. It has been suggested that in Old English this expression denoted an unattended shelter for wayfarers, although in fact some places with this name were of considerable status by 1086, when they appear in Domesday Book. In some instances this and some of the other shortened forms may have arisen from Calcot in Berkshire, Collacott(s) in Devon, or Calcutt in Wiltshire, in all of which the first element apparently comes from the Old English personal name Cola (see Cole 2) or the word col ‘(char)coal’, in which case the meaning would be something like ‘coalshed’.

variants.name: Collignon
meanings: French and Walloon: from a shortened pet form (see Cole and Colle ) of the French personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Colling
meanings: English (northern England and Devon): from the Old Norse personal name Kollungr, a derivative of Koli, or from Old English Colling, a derivative of Cola (see Cole ). Alternatively, from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas (see also Colin ).
German: variant of   Kolling   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Collard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Donat, Normand, Octave, Adelard, Aldor, Andre, Armand, Emile, Germaine, Marcel.
English (southern), French, and Walloon: from a diminutive of the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), a pet form of Nic(h)olas (see Cole ), formed with the Old French suffix -ard.

variants.name: Coale
meanings: English: post-medieval form of   Cole  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Coles
meanings: English (southern): variant of Cole , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Nichol
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from the Middle English personal name Nic(h)ol(e), a vernacular form of Nic(h)olas (see Nicholas ). Compare Nickel and Nicol .

In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’, including Nikolis (see Nicoli ). Compare Nicholas and Nicol .

variants.name: Coalson
meanings: English: patronymic from   Cole  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Cole was born in 1210, in Devon, England, his father, Roger Cole, was 25 and his mother, Edith Burdenville, was 24. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1235, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Cole est né en 1210, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Roger Cole, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Edith Burdenville, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1235, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Cole 1210 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Cole 25 und seine Mutter Edith Burdenville 24. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1235 im Alter von 25 Jahren in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Cole è nato il nel 1210 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, Roger Cole, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Edith Burdenville, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1235, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 25 anni.

ja.Devon, England: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coleが1210年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Roger Coleは25歳，母Edith Burdenvilleは24歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1235年にイギリスイギリスデボンで25歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Cole(이)가 1210년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Cole(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Edith Burdenville(은)는 24세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1235년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Cole nasceu em 1210, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Cole, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Edith Burdenville, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1235, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Cole родился в 1210 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Cole было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Edith Burdenville, 24. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1235 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Cole nació en 1210, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Cole, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Edith Burdenville, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1235, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 25 años.
